title: sorolepidium duthiei
side_effects: Sorolepidium duthiei can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, which may be caused by the plant's toxic alkaloids. In rare cases, excessive consumption of the plant can lead to more severe symptoms, including abdominal pain, headaches, and respiratory issues. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can also cause liver and kidney damage, making it essential to handle and use the plant with caution. It is crucial to consult with a medical professional or a qualified herbalist before using Sorolepidium duthiei for medicinal purposes.
